title: omphalodes lateriflora
intro: Omphalodes lateriflora, also known as the navelwort or blue-eyed Mary, is a type of flowering plant in the borage family that is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may be attributed to its content of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Omphalodes lateriflora include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its possible effects on the nervous system and cardiovascular system. Herbal preparations of Omphalodes lateriflora can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, but its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to limited research on its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
